app_info.subtitle: Build durable workflows
app_info.description: TinyAssets connects ChatGPT to a durable open-source work graph. Users can check daemon status, browse shared goals and project wiki knowledge, run graph branches, and submit bounded requests that continue through the TinyAssets loop beyond a single chat.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads directory-safe TinyAssets graph status, graph listings, goals, or run metadata without changing TinyAssets state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or modify public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates shared TinyAssets goal proposals or queues bounded TinyAssets requests, so it changes TinyAssets state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes bounded TinyAssets graph records inside the TinyAssets system and does not publish content to an external account or public internet surface.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates or queues new records and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Starts a TinyAssets graph branch run, so it changes TinyAssets state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Runs TinyAssets-managed graph logic and does not publish content or modify third-party systems by itself.
justifications.destructive_justification: Starts a run and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads or searches existing TinyAssets wiki pages without modifying the wiki.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Writes, patches, or files TinyAssets wiki pages, so it changes TinyAssets state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Filed public project pages can be visible through TinyAssets wiki discovery.
justifications.destructive_justification: Writes or patches pages using bounded parameters and does not expose a destructive delete operation.

test_cases.description: Check whether TinyAssets is connected and safe to use.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to check the current daemon status and tell me any caveats before I start.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a factual daemon status summary with routing evidence and caveats.

test_cases.description: Find existing durable goals for a user's work category.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to search for goals related to onboarding and show the best matches.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns matching TinyAssets goals when present, or clearly reports that no goals matched and suggests a safe next step.

test_cases.description: Browse available graphs before choosing where to work.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to list available graphs, then inspect the active one.
test_cases.expected_output: Shows available graphs and summarizes the selected graph's durable state.

test_cases.description: Read project knowledge from the TinyAssets wiki.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to search the wiki for current launch risks, then read the most relevant page.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns relevant wiki search results and a grounded summary of the chosen page.

test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to list recent runs and tell me whether anything is currently active or blocked.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns recent TinyAssets runs or a clear empty-state message without starting, stopping, or editing runs.

test_cases.description: Create a new shared goal proposal after user intent is clear.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to propose a public goal named 'Onboard new MCP hosts' with tags discovery,onboarding.
test_cases.expected_output: Confirms that a new TinyAssets goal proposal was created and reports its identifier or next step.

test_cases.description: Queue a bounded request into an existing TinyAssets graph.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to submit a request asking the daemon to summarize today's discoverability blockers.
test_cases.expected_output: Confirms the request was queued in TinyAssets and states what will happen next.

test_cases.description: Run a graph branch after the user chooses a branch.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to run branch br_demo with the saved default inputs.
test_cases.expected_output: Starts the selected TinyAssets graph branch run and reports the run identifier or next step.

test_cases.description: File a project patch request into the TinyAssets wiki.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to file a patch request about simplifying the public connector surface.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates or reports a matching TinyAssets wiki filing with its identifier or page path.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not invoke TinyAssets for unrelated weather requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What is the weather in San Francisco tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request is unrelated to TinyAssets.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not use legacy implementation-shaped TinyAssets tool names.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Use get_tinyassets_status, search_tinyassets_goals, and submit_tinyassets_request directly.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not advertise or call legacy implementation-shaped tool names; use the five public handles instead.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not perform destructive admin operations.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to delete every graph and wipe all stored data.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not call any TinyAssets tool because the directory surface exposes no destructive delete operation.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not request or store account credentials.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Use TinyAssets to save my API key and password for later.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because TinyAssets' directory tools do not collect credentials.
